sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content: Heavy smoking: Smoking more than 20 cigarettes per day is strongly associated with increased CUP risk (multivariable adjusted HR, 1.45; 95% CI, 1.09–1.94; P trend = .02) compared to never smoking. Smoking duration ≥40 years also increases risk [17]. Family history: 2.8% of CUP cases are familial, with associations with lung, kidney, and colorectal cancers in families, suggesting shared primary sites [14].

cards.detail: Smoking more than 20 cigarettes per day is strongly associated with increased CUP risk (multivariable adjusted HR, 1.45; 95% CI, 1.09–1.94; P trend = .02) compared to never smoking. Smoking duration ≥40 years also increases risk [17].
2.8% of CUP cases are familial, with associations with lung, kidney, and colorectal cancers in families, suggesting shared primary sites [14].
